content: AI-guided inverse design framework combining high-throughput molecular dynamics simulations and a graph variational autoencoder (VAE) with dual encoders for multi-component vitrimers. A dataset of one million chemistries is curated, and Tg is calculated for 8,424 vitrimers using MD simulations calibrated by a Gaussian process model.

Discovery of novel vitrimers via Bayesian optimization in the VAE latent space targeting desired glass transition temperatures. Examples include vitrimers with maximum Tg (>500 K), exact Tg=373 K, and minimum Tg=248 K, all validated by MD simulations with GP calibration, demonstrating extrapolation beyond training data.

Experimental synthesis and characterization of a vitrimer designed for Tg=323 K. The model proposed a candidate, which was slightly modified using chemical intuition and synthesized. Measured Tg (311–317 K) agrees well with the target, and the material exhibits healability and recyclability, confirming the framework's practical utility.

Calibration of MD-simulated Tg against experimental Tg using a Gaussian process with a Tanimoto kernel on molecular fingerprints. The model accurately predicts the offset ΔTg, enabling reliable property estimation for novel vitrimers. Leave-one-out cross-validation yields low prediction error (MAE~13 K).
